posts.text:
I'm glad this story is getting attention but per usual the thing about this administration is that they flat-out say the awful things they're going to do before they do them. They published the plan for the America First Global Health Policy months ago, and it just... says this.


Justin Wolfers (@JustinWolfers)

Things are not okay.





— https://nitter.net/JustinWolfers/status/2033661772909961700#m

Texas was blessed with two great Democratic candidates in this primary, but I'm really glad to see Talarico win. A reminder that acting with decency, integrity, and generosity can still win in American politics. 
slate.com/news-and-politics/…



Michael Lange (@MichaelLangeNYC)

This is Travis County (Austin), holy shit.

Talarico earning 90%+ in some precincts, 80%+ in the vast majority of the West Side. Blockbuster support from White voters of all ages, incomes, urban/rural.

These are Mamdani

Also I tweeted this because I flew to Nairobi yesterday and had a minor medical issue that gave me, like, 22 minutes to pack like a schizophrenic raccoon. I have absolutely no idea what's in my luggage, but I know for sure I have the bare minimum of what I need because my carry-on and my toiletries kit are always pre-packed. I might have 26 pairs of socks and only one shoe in my luggage, but I do have a silk eye mask and ear plugs.

Bonus advice: Have a toiletries kit pre-packed too, that you can stick in your checked or carry-on luggage. Mini shampoo/conditioner, serum, moisturizer, sunscreen, face wash, deodorant, razor (might get taken tho), toothbrush, small toothpaste, comb / small hairbrush, mascara. Also a good spot for those Sephora free samples. If you use $$$$ skincare, put cheaper but still fine stuff in the perma-packed kit, you can always add your good stuff to it, but this way you know you'll have all your bas

Have your travel carry-on perma-packed. Power adapter with cords that go to each of your devices. Headphones. Eye mask. Ear plugs. A scarf. Pens. Never unpack it.


Tokyo (@otokyo__)

I’m 25. Give me oddly specific life tips. No general ”surround yourself with positive people” tips. I want the most random, specific advice possible.



— https://nitter.net/otokyo__/status/2028297741592387655#m

Texas Democrats are very lucky to be choosing between two strong candidates. But each one offers a very different vision of what wins elections (rally the base vs. persuade and convert), and of how a Democratic politician should be in this moment:
slate.com/news-and-politics/…
54% of male Republicans under the age of 50 believe "the Holocaust was greatly exaggerated or did not happen as historians describe." manhattan.institute/article/the-...
A small, influential group of right-wingers say their role model, Joseph McCarthy, didn't go far enough, @alibreland.bsky.social reports:
Rivers are living beings.

Might write longer on this at some point but the McDonald’s CEO barely being able to eat the trash food he sells to poorer people around the world is Big Food / fast food in a nutshell: people getting very wealthy selling food they’d never eat to someone else’s kids.

"Fire hurts," complains arsonist

www.texastribune.org/2026/03/06/d...
Read @jillfilipovic.bsky.social please: (5/?)

ICE has arrested and detained a Nashville journalist who reported stories critical of ICE. She’s married to a U.S. citizen and has been seeking asylum here after fleeing death threats in Colombia because of her journalism there. 

They’ve already sent her to Louisiana.

Reuters Exclusive

"U.S. military investigators believe it is likely that U.S. forces were responsible for an apparent strike on an Iranian girls' school."

"The strike would rank among the worst cases of civilian casualties in decades of U.S. conflicts in the ​Middle East."

Perhaps unsurprising for Miami, but I still find it notable that the three students quoted with most sexist, bigoted and antisemitic texts are named Bejerano, Gonzalez, and Valdes.

As someone said recently, America is such a melting pot that these days even our white supremacists are multicultural.

The secretary of Miami-Dade County’s GOP started a group chat for conservative students — and within 3 weeks, it was filled with over 400 instances of the N-word, Nazi rhetoric and writings of "dozens of ways of violently killing Black people"

None of this is to say that the New York Times doesn’t have a general perspective. But I’m fairly confident that if you did a survey of the political leanings of every opinion piece published over the last decade, you’d see that perspective is very much on the liberal side of things.

This is not actually how it works. Op/ed editors at the Times and other publications publish all sorts of opinions they don't agree with. The idea that they only pick pieces because they support the opinion is... stunningly ignorant.

That is... just demonstrably false. 
www.nytimes.com/2023/06/13/o...

www.nytimes.com/2025/12/11/o...

www.nytimes.com/2019/08/03/o...

www.nytimes.com/interactive/...
What has the New York Times published knowing it was false? They don't always get the story right, and they issue corrections when they're wrong, but that's a pretty weighty claim to make.

They have two conservative columnists on staff, and many more liberal ones. I disagree with Douthat and Stephens, but I also honestly don't see the problem with a national newspaper including what are honestly fairly mainstream conservative voices. The "intent" is to rep a variety of views.
Seems like a thing more people should be mad about
I mean, yes, that is the job of a newspaper op/ed page.

The last time I worked as an editor on a newspaper opinion desk was in college but yeah I published all kinds of opinions I didn't agree with! That's the job. Again, if you don't comprehend that, that's a media literacy problem on your part, and it might help to learn about how newspapers work.

Criticize the opinions! That's fair game. But suggesting that "The New York Times" as an entity agrees with the opinion of every op/ed they publish is deranged and in fact makes no sense if you think about it for more than two seconds.
